mechanism: Proposed mechanism: kratom contains indole/oxindole alkaloids; mitragynine is described as a major alkaloid and is metabolized to 7-hydroxymitragynine, which is more potent at μ-opioid receptor activity in mechanistic discussions; human evidence remains limited.
activeCompounds: 7-hydroxymitragynine (7‑OH) (alkaloids discussed in mechanistic literature and noted in FDA enforcement focus).
effects: No direct effects data. Contextual inference:

contraindications: Avoid during pregnancy and breastfeeding unless supervised by a qualified clinician.
Do not use with known allergy to this plant or related species.
safetyNotes: Potential for dependence/withdrawal; reported serious adverse events include seizures and liver problems; deaths appear linked mainly to use in combination with other drugs; liver injury cases have been reported in structured case series.
description: A tropical tree; POWO reports native range from S. Indo-China to New Guinea. In the U.S., products have been marketed as "dietary supplements," but legality is contested by FDA statements.

interactions: NCCIH flags that kratom may interact with many medications and that more research is needed on drug interactions; NCCIH also notes combination with other drugs has been linked to severe adverse effects/deaths.

region: Native range (POWO): S. Indo-China to New Guinea.
therapeuticUses: Traditional supportive use; indication depends on species part and preparation.
Evidence level varies; use only within established clinical or traditional context.
sideEffects: May cause gastrointestinal upset (nausea, cramping, or diarrhea).
Possible headache, dizziness, or allergic skin reaction in sensitive users.
summary: Research is early; NCCIH notes major knowledge gaps about short- and long-term effects and safety. Reported uses include attempts to manage opioid withdrawal, but kratom can be addictive with withdrawal symptoms; adverse effects range from nausea/constipation/drowsiness to rare serious effects (seizures, high blood pressure, liver problems).
